content:
Hi
3. managed it - was ok for me - still an early clasher but  I have already seen clashes way 'tighter' I couldn't finish (e.g printing words crossed at earliest common vowel)- posting my initial sol passing the tests as a hint for how others tackle this

m=n=int(input())
while m<10:m+=n
l=m//n
s=[*map(str,range(1,10))]+['0']+['X']*(m-10)
b=(2*n+1)*'*'
print(b)
for i in range(l):
    print('*'+'*'.join(s[i*n:(i+1)*n])+'*')
    print(b)

Could you guys direct any feedback to these concerns below? Thank you so much!
1. How can I add the constraint of n>0 as a comment to the input.
(ex. n=int(input()) # 0<n≤20)
2. Should my WRITE statement just be something like "write answer," or is the one I have okay?
3. BIG ONE: Try to solve this clash (without copying my solution). If I were to assume, the pattern is probably easily recognizable, but the programming might take a while. Did it take you less than 15 minutes? Could you even solve it? I just want to make sure this puzzle is novice-medium difficulty.
4. If there is anything else that I am missing (like edge test cases), feel free to point it out!

Here are my answers to some of your questions.

Q1. You can use the INPUT keyword. Refer to the explanation here: ( https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/stub-generator ) .

Q2. The write statement you have is fine.

Q4. You've done a good job in identifying all the edge test cases. However, I don't think the test case with n = 20 is an edge case (hitting the upper bound of the constraint doesn't necessarily make it one). Please change the value of n in one of the n = 20 cases to another value, e.g. 19.

Also, although it's not required for the Reverse mode, please format "n" as a [[variable]] in the Constraints section 😉

Thanks.
I wrote 'early clasher' but I meant recent clasher

Just wondering, how quickly did you spot the pattern? (instantly, took some counting, or spent about 5 minutes scanning for one) Also, did you solve this in under 15 minutes?

Thanks for your feedback!
Thanks so much for your help :)
